rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the types of their assigned expressions.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced features and variables are declared or exist in the current AADL scope.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE const and eq declarations to avoid resolution errors.
Validate that guarantee expressions have valid types and do not reference undefined identifiers.
Place AGREE annexes in appropriate component scopes: type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; avoid '<error>' types by ensuring all identifiers are properly declared.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package references or qualified names not present in the AADL model dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assignments and equations there.
Do not reference undefined identifiers in AGREE expressions without first declaring them in the same annex.
irrelevant_topics: component type vs implementation scope distinctions
